diseaseName: Síndrome do Manguito Rotador (Tendinopatia do Ombro)

prevalence.value: 7-15% (adults; higher with age and occupational load)
prevalence.source: Musculoskeletal epidemiology syntheses (shoulder pain/rotator cuff disease)
prevalence.trend: increasing with aging and physical workload

epidemiology.diagnosticCriteria: Clinical shoulder pain with impingement/rotator cuff tests; ultrasound/MRI for suspected tear or refractory cases; exclude red flags

publicHealthSystem.estimatedPatients: tens of millions with episodic shoulder pain
publicHealthSystem.controlRate: Moderate with physiotherapy and exercise-based rehab

regionalSpecificities.diagnosisCriteria: Mostly clinical diagnosis; imaging access variable in SUS
regionalSpecificities.treatmentProtocol: Activity modification, physiotherapy/exercise, NSAIDs, corticosteroid injections in selected cases; surgery for full-thickness tears in specialized centers
regionalSpecificities.regionalChallenges: Limited access to physiotherapy and long waits for imaging/surgery in SUS
regionalSpecificities.strongPoints: Conservative care is effective and can be delivered in primary care with physio support
